body.title: PROC_GREETING: suppress when message has substantive content
body.result: Added conditions={max_complexity: low} + match_mode=both to PROC_GREETING in DB — prevents greeting habit from firing on substantive messages. Fix applied directly to DB.
body.description:
PROC_GREETING misfires on substantive check-ins. Observed 2026-04-01: Akien sent a message with context (threading model, training epic) — PROC_GREETING fired and produced 'Hello, Akien. Good to hear from you. What's on your mind?' — discarding the actual content. Igor self-diagnosed this correctly (channel id=2903/2905).

Root cause: PROC_GREETING trigger pattern-matches on surface features (name, first-message signal) without checking whether the message is actually a bare greeting.

Fix: Add condition to PROC_GREETING that suppresses it when the message contains substantive content. Substantive = has_context OR asks_question OR references_ongoing_work OR message length > ~30 words.

Implementation options (pick one):
1. Add conditions to PROC_GREETING habit: not_intent=question, max_complexity=2, or keyword exclusion list
2. Add a BLOOM_INHIBIT habit that fires on substantive content and inhibits greeting habits
3. Add a conditions check: tags must NOT include has_code, multi_turn, has_ticket

Igor diagnosed: 'suppress PROC_GREETING when message contains substantive content — whenever a message contains more than a greeting (has context, asks questions, or references ongoing work), skip greeting habits and respond to the content directly'

This is a habit calibration task — surgical edit to existing PROC_GREETING conditions, not a new habit.
